summary: Design-synthesis for the Tequila Tao deaddiction app revamp: 3 independent product/design proposals judged & merged into one buildable spec, plus drafted Guides/Privacy/Terms/About copy

spec.name: Tequila Tao
spec.tagline: Two forces. One you. Find your balance, one clear night at a time.
spec.oneLineVision: A private, offline, on-device companion that turns the yin-yang into a living, breathing portrait of your relationship with drinking, replacing an opaque six-figure point blob with legible real-world wins (streak, money kept, drinks avoided, urges surfed, clarity trend) wrapped in a calm, witty, never-shaming Taoist aesthetic.
spec.metaphorAndWinState: Yin = clarity, recovery, restraint, rest, money kept, drinks avoided; Yang = celebration, indulgence, the nights you choose to drink. The yin-yang is NOT a scoreboard and NOT a purity meter: an all-yin circle is explicitly framed as out of balance too, and a thin seed of yang always remains because the Taoist goal is mindful agency, not forced abstinence. WIN STATE = the user steers toward THEIR self-chosen target ratio (set in onboarding, e.g. 85/15) and recovers gracefully from any slip; the win is the chord moving toward the dashed target marker and the comeback, never a fully white circle. Decisive resolution of the three proposals: we adopt Proposal 1's explicit user-chosen target ratio plus Proposal 3's earned-serenity visual language (a soft S-curve divider that settles toward center over consistent days, plus a gold rim patina arc accruing one segment per milestone), and Proposal 2's gentle game layer (Freezes, levels, collectibles) strictly subordinated to the real numbers. The two seed-dots are reframed and interactive (Proposal 3): the light dot inside yang = the one clear thing you keep even on a big night (water between drinks / a safe ride), the dark dot inside yin = permission to celebrate, you are allowed joy. No shame state exists in the visual or copy vocabulary; a heavy night softens the curve toward yang gently and copy stays curious (Big night. Let us land it well tomorrow). The app never says you failed; it says the balance shifted, what do you want next.

economy.action: Finish onboarding (set intention + honest baseline + target ratio)
economy.effect: Writes profile to localStorage. The yin-yang seeds to your REAL starting tilt (computed from baseline drinks/week, never a fake 50/50). Target chord marker is placed at your chosen ratio. You unlock the first collectible, The First Breath card.
economy.rationale: Anchoring the economy to the user's actual life makes every downstream number (money, drinks) feel true and earned. Immediate reward drives first-session retention (Fogg).

economy.action: Log a Clear Night (chose not to drink when you might have) via Crossroads then Clarity
economy.effect: +1 Clear-Night streak; +N Drinks Avoided (N = your typical session size, default 3, editable); your typical spend is banked to Money Kept (prefilled from baseline, editable per night); the Yin field rises one visible fixed notch and the chord springs toward target; soft koi-pond chime + light haptic. All four numbers shown live before you confirm.
economy.rationale: One legible action maps to four legible, permanent, real-world motivators with no hidden multipliers. Money saved and drinks avoided are the two strongest documented motivators in moderation apps; the streak is the emotional engine.

economy.action: Ride out a craving with Urge Surf (Stillpoint) and come out without drinking
economy.effect: +1 Urges Surfed (lifetime + this-week, the pride metric); logs urge intensity before AND after (1-10) so you SEE it fall (e.g. 8 to 3) on a tiny chart; adds a small Yin notch (~0.5 of a Clear Night); optional one-line note. This is the single most-rewarded action in the whole economy.
economy.rationale: Urge-surfing (Marlatt): urges crest and pass like waves in ~20 min. Showing measured intensity drop is direct proof the skill works, building self-efficacy, the #1 predictor of maintained change. Rewarding it most heavily signals what the app values.

economy.action: Choose to drink and log it honestly (Crossroads then Send-off)
economy.effect: NO shame screen, NO red. Clear-Night streak PAUSES (not zeroed): best streak is preserved and a fresh count begins. 2 free Freezes per week mean a planned night need not even pause it. Logs drinks + spend so trends stay honest. Yang field rises proportionally. App offers a one-tap Harm-Reduction Kit (water between rounds, set a limit, eat first, safe ride) and the tappable keep-one-clear-thing promise. Lifetime Money Kept / Drinks Avoided are untouched.
economy.rationale: Honesty must be safe. Punishing honest logging teaches users to lie or churn. Preserving best-streak and never decrementing lifetime totals directly counters the Abstinence Violation Effect, the core of relapse-prevention design.

economy.action: Complete a Recover (Soft Landing) morning-after session
economy.effect: +1 Soft Landing; adds a Yin notch (self-care literally grows clarity; the yin field fills as you tick items); folds in a mood/clarity check-in (1-5) feeding the trend; copy stays gentle (Rough morning is data, not failure). Notches are for the self-care, never for the drinking.
economy.rationale: Self-compassion practices reduce relapse. Reframing the morning-after from damage control to a legitimate Yin act keeps heavy-night users engaged instead of dropping off in guilt.

economy.action: Log a daily mood/clarity check-in (one tap, 5 faces)
economy.effect: Paints one tile on the Clarity Garden heatmap; feeds the 7-day Clarity sparkline; keeps your daily check-in streak alive EVEN on drink nights (showing up counts). No penalty for a missed day.
economy.rationale: Decoupling the engagement streak from the sobriety streak means a slip never resets your relationship with the app, only one counter. Mood-over-time is the visible proof that reducing drinking is working.

economy.action: Hit a Clear-Night milestone (3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 100) or a Money/Drinks threshold
economy.effect: The rim gains one gold patina arc segment; a single tasteful bloom ripple plays once (no confetti); a milestone card animates in showing REAL aggregates (7 clear nights, Rs.X kept, Y drinks avoided) with an editable real-world equivalence (Rs.10,000 ~ the weekend trip you wanted) and an exportable share-card image. Level title advances: Sprout to Stream to Grove to Mountain to Still Lake.
economy.rationale: Variable-ratio celebration at meaningful REAL thresholds sustains motivation and converts raw streak into identity and pride. Tying it to a self-chosen reward goal makes the abstract concrete; the share card drives organic, non-outing growth.

economy.action: Break a streak / relapse / miss a day
economy.effect: NO red, no you failed. A calm Welcome back, the water's fine screen: Slips are data, not defeat. Your 14 best days still happened. One tap restarts the Clear-Night counter at Day 1 with an optional What did I learn note, and you earn a gentle Back to the Water collectible. Daily check-in streak, Money Kept, Drinks Avoided, Urges Surfed and best-streak are all untouched. A broken streak animates to soft slate, never alarm-red.
economy.rationale: Relapse-resilience IS the game. Lifetime cumulative counters that only ever grow give users something a slip cannot erase, and reframing return as part of the path is the single strongest anti-shame touch.

routes.name: Threshold (Intro / Splash)
routes.purpose: First-touch brand moment and entry. No login (no-accounts constraint). Routes returning users (localStorage profile present) straight to /home.
keyElements: Live SVG breathing yin-yang centered, drawn instantly (no image dependency, no layout shift)

Optional lazy-loaded ambient intro accent (existing concept.mp4) behind the SVG, poster-first, decode async, loads only on first visit after idle; prefers-reduced-motion shows a static wine-to-noir gradient, no video
Wordmark Tequila Tao (Playfair Display) + tagline
Single CTA Begin to /onboarding on first run, else auto-skip to /home
Microcopy No account. Nothing leaves your phone. + tiny Restore from backup (import JSON) link for returning users on a new device

routes.name: The Way In (Guide + Baseline)
routes.purpose: Under-60s, fully skippable setup that teaches the metaphor and captures the honest baseline powering every real number. Behavioral: implementation intentions.
keyElements: 4 swipeable cards with progress dots; Skip for now on every card applies sensible defaults and drops to /home
Card 1 intent chips (single pick): Cut back / Take a break / Quit (tunes language only, never judgment)
Card 2 baseline: drinks/week slider (0-30) + typical spend per night + currency, with live preview (a skipped 3-drink night = +Rs.1,050 kept)
Card 3 target ratio: drag the chord on a LIVE yin-yang to choose your target (e.g. 85/15), establishing the goal is YOUR balance, not forced 100%

Card 4 your why + reward goal (Rs.10k = ___) for money equivalence, plus one implementation intention (WHEN trigger, I WILL plan) seeded as the default Tonight reminder; privacy line + link to /privacy
Finish Hatch your Tao: disc animates to the seeded tilt, lands on /home with target marker and first card earned. Writes localStorage key tt_profile_v1, onboarded true

routes.name: Center (Home / Dashboard)
routes.purpose: Calm command center. The living yin-yang plus first-class motivators at a glance and two clear entry actions. Replaces the old 3-zone video dashboard.
keyElements: Large breathing SVG yin-yang centered, soft S-curve divider at lifetime ratio, dashed target chord overlaid, gold patina rim arcs for milestones; tap to /balance
Balance read line: Centered, leaning clear, Yin 82% / target 85 + (i) opening the transparent formula
Stat row (4 pill cards): Clear-Night Streak (Day 12, best beneath), Money Kept (Rs.7,200), Drinks Avoided (38), Urges Surfed (9), all tabular-nums
Two large tactile actions: Tonight (to /crossroads) and Morning after (to /recover)
Persistent Urge SOS button, always reachable, gently pulses ONLY when a recently logged urge was high (to /urge)
One-tap 5-face mood check-in strip if not done today; 7-day Clarity sparkline + 7-day dot strip (clear / drank / rest)
Bottom tab bar: Home, Garden (/progress), Apothecary, Settings; safe-area padded; Tao mascot quip in a corner

routes.name: Crossroads (Tonight's choice)
routes.purpose: Reimagined Conundrum: the nightly decision, calm, non-coercive, with a pause + intention.
keyElements: Opening urge check How are you feeling about tonight? slider 1-10; if 6 or higher, gentle nudge Want 3 minutes first? to /urge

Two equal-weight panels (no guilt styling): Not tonight (yin/ink) and I am having some (yang/amber), each with a live consequence preview (Not tonight gives streak 8, +Rs.1,050 kept / Having some logs it, land it well)
Shows tonight's implementation intention from onboarding as a reminder
I am having some opens a drink-count stepper (1-10) + optional who/where chip to /sendoff; Not tonight gives a ripple to /clarity
Not sure link routes to /urge, meeting ambivalence with a tool, never pressure

routes.name: Clarity (Bank the win)
routes.purpose: Capture the concrete, permanent reward of choosing not to drink.
keyElements: How much would tonight have cost? amount input prefilled from baseline + currency; drinks-avoided prefilled from profile, both editable
Live preview: That is +Rs.600 saved, +3 drinks avoided, +1 to your streak
Confirm triggers disc-fills-with-light animation + yin chime + counters update + a chance of a collectible card draw
Supportive line Clear choice. Banked. to /balance (or milestone celebration if a threshold crossed)

routes.name: Send-off + Harm-Reduction Kit
routes.purpose: The I-am-having-some path: warm, celebratory but mindful and harm-reducing, never preachy or shaming.
keyElements: Amber gradient scene; ONE optional lazy-loaded short celebratory accent (existing party.mp4), muted, playsInline, loads only here
Logs the drink count from Crossroads + spend; +honesty acknowledgment; one-tap Freeze my streak showing Freezes remaining
Harm-Reduction Kit toggles: water between rounds, set a limit of N, eat first, safe ride home; the tappable yang seed-dot keep one clear thing tonight promise that glows until morning
Sets a soft drink-limit reminder (local-notification-ready for Capacitor); best-streak-preserved badge
Mascot: Have a good one. I will be here in the morning, no judgment. to /home

routes.name: Stillpoint (Urge Surf / SOS)
routes.purpose: The behavior-change heart: ride out a craving in real time. Most-rewarded action; always reachable; works offline.
keyElements: Pre-rating: How strong is the urge? 1-10

Full-screen breathing yin-yang as a box / 4-7-8 pacer (the idle breathing asset doing double duty), 3-5 min wave timer with rising-then-falling wave + urges crest in ~20 min countdown, skippable to 90s
Rotating grounding microcopy (You are the surfer, not the wave; Name 3 things you can hear); distraction quick-links (call someone, walk, water)
Post-rating: urge now? shows the drop (8 to 3) on a small chart + optional note

Outcome: Made it, did not drink (+Urge Surfed, Yin notch) or I am choosing to drink (to /sendoff, zero shame). prefers-reduced-motion: pacer becomes a calm numeric/opacity cue + progress bar, no scaling

routes.name: Soft Landing (Morning after)
routes.purpose: Reframed, de-jargoned hangover-recovery checklist + honest reflection, gamified gently as self-care, never scolding.
keyElements: Vertical tactile checklist with the why one-liner each: Hydrate, Box-breathing (inline 60s 4-4-4-4 guide), Light fuel, Caffeine timing, Safety check, optional Cooling/Movement
Each tick fills a progress ring + soft chime; the yin field gently fills as you complete (self-care grows clarity)
Safety-first card stays prominent (severe withdrawal symptoms, seek care); this is the ONE place alarm-amber is allowed
End reflection: How do you feel now? 1-5 faces + optional last night cost roughly stepper feeding Money/this-month + optional what I learned
Completes to +1 Soft Landing, Yin notch, then /balance

routes.name: Balance (The Tao view)
routes.purpose: Full-screen meditative deep-dive with FULL transparency of the rules, the antidote to the old opaque point economy.
keyElements: Hero animated yin-yang: soft S-curve divider, current chord + dashed target chord, milestone patina arcs
Plain-language read Leaning clear, Yin 82 / Yang 18, target 85, you are in your zone + one line on what moved it most recently
Transparency accordion How this is calculated: lists every input and its exact step (Clear night +3 yin, Drink +1 yang each, Urge surfed +1 yin, Soft landing +1 yin)
Stat tiles: current vs best streak, Money Kept (lifetime + this month), Drinks Avoided, Urges Surfed, 30-day Clarity trend sparkline
Reset is not failure note + link to adjust goal in Settings; calm swipe-down / cross-fade exit to /home, never a swirl

routes.name: Clarity Garden (Progress & Trends)
routes.purpose: Long-game retention surface: every motivator first-class and inspectable over time, plus the collection.
keyElements: Clarity Garden heatmap calendar: each check-in paints a tile, clear nights bloom (teal to jade to gold), drink nights neutral slate (never red), rest days sage
Lifetime totals with a these numbers never go down badge: money kept, drinks avoided, urges surfed, longest streak
Money-saved goal thermometer toward the user's reward (Rs.7,200 of Rs.10,000 to weekend trip)
Urge intensity-over-time chart proving urges fall; mood vs yin% trend
Clarity Card collection grid (collectibles with rarities); export/share milestone cards; all read from local data, nothing leaves the device

routes.name: Apothecary (Cosmetic shop)
routes.purpose: Transparent, optional cosmetic sink for Clarity Points. Never pay-to-win, never real money, never gates a health feature.
keyElements: Grid of disc skins (Moonstone, Ember, Jade), mascot outfits, sound packs with clear Clarity-Point prices and owned/locked states
Live preview applies a skin to a sample disc via CSS variables (geometry never changes)
Note: Everything here is cosmetic. Your recovery is never for sale.
Skippable / entirely ignorable; not required to use any core flow

routes.name: Settings
routes.purpose: Control, privacy, and data ownership (hard constraint).
keyElements: Edit baseline, target ratio, reward goal, currency, typical session size, intentions
Reminders: daily check-in time + drink-limit nudge (Capacitor-ready local notifications); ambient sound (default OFF), haptics, reduced-motion override toggles
DATA: Export my data (JSON), Import backup, Soft Reset streak (preserves lifetime totals), Clear everything (double-confirm)
Install as app (PWA) button when beforeinstallprompt is available; theme; links to /guide, /privacy, /terms, app version

routes.name: Guide / How It Works
routes.purpose: Required guide page; explains the transparent rules and metaphor anytime.
keyElements: The metaphor in one screen (Yin/Yang, why not 100%, the seed-dots)
Exactly how streaks, money kept, drinks avoided, urge-surfing, Freezes and the balance math work, in real numbers
Slips are data relapse-resilience section; how to use Urge SOS
Crisis resources / helpline links (region-aware placeholder) with a clear this app is not medical care note

routes.name: Privacy Policy
routes.purpose: Required. Reinforces the on-device, no-account promise.
keyElements: Plain-English: No accounts. No servers. No tracking. Everything lives in your browser/device storage.
What is stored (localStorage/IndexedDB keys listed), how to export/delete
No third-party analytics statement; contact + last-updated date

routes.name: Terms & Care Notice
routes.purpose: Required. Legal + genuine health safety net.
keyElements: Not a medical device; not a substitute for professional treatment; as-is no-warranty
Emergency guidance (severe withdrawal can be dangerous, seek care) prominent
Crisis & help resources block (region-aware links); acceptable use; user owns their data; back to /home

dashboard: Clear-Night Streak: current run of nights not drinking, with best-ever preserved beneath (e.g. Day 12, best 21); pauses (never zeroes) on a drink night
Money Kept: real currency total banked, computed from the user's own per-night spend (e.g. Rs.7,200), tappable to the reward-goal thermometer, carries a never goes down badge
Drinks Avoided: lifetime count of drinks you would typically have had on skipped nights (e.g. 38 drinks)
Urges Surfed: lifetime count of urges ridden out with Stillpoint (e.g. 9), the pride metric
Balance Read: plain-language state of the yin-yang with live Yin% and target marker (e.g. 82% Yin, target 85, leaning clear), tappable to /balance
Clarity Trend: 7-day mood/clarity sparkline from one-tap check-ins (feeling-better as proof the change is working)
Today's status dot: checked-in / clear / drank / rest on a 7-day strip; rest day is a first-class, celebrated sage dot
spec.onboarding: First run (after the / Threshold Begin CTA) goes to /onboarding: under 60 seconds, fully skippable, 4 swipeable cards with progress dots, stated up front that no account is needed and nothing leaves the device. Card 1: pick intent (Cut back / Take a break / Quit) which only tunes the app's language, never adds judgment. Card 2: honest baseline (drinks/week slider 0-30 and typical spend per night + currency) with a live preview (a skipped 3-drink night = +Rs.1,050 kept) so the economy reflects real life from minute one instead of a fake 50/50. Card 3: drag the chord on a live yin-yang to set your TARGET ratio (e.g. 85/15), establishing that the goal is YOUR chosen balance, not forced abstinence. Card 4: name your why + a reward goal so Rs.10,000 saved maps to something you actually want, and build one implementation intention (WHEN trigger, I WILL if-then plan) seeded as the default Tonight reminder, the single most evidence-backed habit-change technique. Defaults are pre-filled and Set this later is on every card, so a user can tap through in seconds; everything is editable in Settings. Finishing Hatch your Tao animates the disc to the seeded tilt, wakes the mascot, awards the first collectible (The First Breath), writes tt_profile_v1 to localStorage, and lands on /home with the target marker already showing. We never ask for name, email, or anything identifying.
quirkyHooks: The yin-yang breathes at ~6 breaths/min (5s in / 5s out) on idle: a stealth box-breathing pacer hiding as a logo, and it IS the same asset that powers the Urge Surf timer. One asset, two jobs; match it and you cannot help but calm down.

Tao the mascot: a tiny animated yin-yang spirit whose two seed-dots are its eyes. Dry, warm, never preachy. Rotating quips: Water does not try to flow, it just does / I counted your clear nights, I lost count, good problem / on a drink night: No lecture, drink water, sleep well, see you tomorrow.

Earned serenity you can SEE: streak milestones do not spew confetti, they age the symbol, accruing a thin gold patina arc on the rim like respect on old brass, and the S-curve divider settles toward center over consistent days.

The seed-dots are interactive: tap the yang dot on /sendoff to make one clear promise for the night (water, a safe ride) and it glows faintly until morning; long-press the yin dot for a rotating Taoist/recovery koan (The soft overcomes the hard), fully offline, never gamified into a grind.
Money Kept is playfully reframed on tap: That is 4 great coffees / a concert ticket / 0.3 of a flight home (configurable, gently funny), tied to the user's own reward goal.

Collectible Clarity Cards on wins with rarities and witty flavor text (Common Tall Glass of Water, Rare The 2am Decline, Legendary Still Lake at 100 nights), plus a special gentle Back to the Water card when a relapse is logged honestly, reframing return as part of the path.
Level titles follow the calming Taoist water/landscape arc: Sprout to Stream to Grove to Mountain to Still Lake, never macho.
No streak-anxiety red anywhere: a broken streak animates to soft slate, drink nights are neutral, true alarm-red is reserved exclusively for medical-safety warnings in Recover/Terms.

Sound is OFF by default and restrained: a soft koi-pond plip on each clear-night log, a single zen-bowl ding on level-up, one soft tide wave on a milestone then silence; a cheeky Tequila Sunrise sound pack unlocks at 30 nights (a wink at the name).

The export file is named like a keepsake: tequila-tao-balance-YYYY-MM-DD.json; milestone share cards auto-generate a beautiful exportable image (30 clear nights, Rs.36,000 kept) that does not out it as a recovery app unless the user wants it to.

visualSystem.palette: Warmed, premium dark base (no pure #000/#FFF in chrome). Background ink #0B0E1A to #141A2E gradient; surfaces #16161C with translucent white/5 to white/12 glass + backdrop-blur. Yin field: warm paper-pearl #F4F1E8 (not clinical) with moonstone accent #BFD7EA and jade success #6FCF97 for positive actions/money. Yang field: brand wine #800020 deepened, gradient to warm ember #E07A5F / amber #C8742A (warm, never neon); worst-case heavy night = deeper amber #A85A22, never red. Milestone/patina gold #D4AF37 reserved ONLY for celebration. Neutral/paused = calm slate #64748B (a broken streak goes slate). Rest day = sage #7C9A82. Garden bloom scale: slate (neutral/drink) to teal #3DCCC7 to jade #6FCF97 to gold. True alarm red is avoided in habit UI and reserved exclusively for medical-safety warnings (Recover/Terms). Text #ECEAE3 primary, rgba(236,234,227,0.7) secondary.
visualSystem.typography: Display/wordmark & emotional headings: Playfair Display (already in the brand), used sparingly, max one display element per screen. UI/body: Inter, 15-17px base, generous ~1.6 line-height for night-time/hungover readability. All metrics/numbers use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums so streak/money/drink counters do not jitter while animating. Mascot quips in Inter italic. DROP Bebas Neue PARTY MODE shouty styling from core UI (reads as pro-drinking hype, off-tone); keep it at most as an optional accent on /sendoff only.
visualSystem.motion: Framer Motion throughout, slow and weighted: default ease cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), durations 400-700ms, nothing snappy. Springs ONLY for the yin-yang divider (stiffness ~120-140, damping ~22-24) so it settles like liquid; reuse the existing spring in YinYang.jsx. The 5s-in/5s-out breathing loop is the heartbeat; the disc fills with light on a clear-night log; a gentle scale+glow bloom (NO confetti) on level-up; number counters roll up with tabular-nums; light haptic tick on choices, soft double on milestones. Page transitions are gentle 0.4-0.6s cross-fade + ~8px y-rise; DELETE the current 720-1080deg full-screen swirls (nausea-inducing, ignore reduced-motion). ALL motion gated behind a single useReducedMotion() hook / prefers-reduced-motion: breathing stops at mid-expansion, transitions become 150ms opacity-only, the pacer becomes a numeric/opacity cue + progress bar, no parallax/scaling. Everything honors safe-area insets and 100svh.
visualSystem.yinYangTreatment: One reusable inline SVG component <YinYang value target breath patina skin onSeedTap/> (viewBox 0 0 200 200), fully vector, pin-sharp 360px to desktop and as the app icon. KEEP and upgrade the existing exact-area chord bisection in YinYang.jsx (mathematically correct, resolution-independent). Upgrades: replace the straight chord with a soft Bezier S-curve divider whose control points are driven by the yin/yang ratio (more yang = curve bows toward yang), animated via Framer Motion path interpolation with the spring; add the two classic seed-dots (one per lobe) as individually addressable, gently counter-pulsing circles that double as the mascot eyes and the interactive clear-promise / koan targets; overlay a dashed target-ratio chord; milestone patina is an SVG rim stroke-dasharray arc filling one #D4AF37 segment per milestone; soft inner shadow + thin pearl rim for depth; idle 1.0 to 1.04 breathing scale. Skins re-tint the two halves and dots via CSS variables only, never altering geometry. Optional canvas particle shimmer ONLY on milestone moments, lazy and reduced-motion-aware. The same component serves Threshold, Home, Balance, Crossroads and the Urge pacer.

spec.mediaUsage: KEEP as lazy optional accents only (the crisp SVG yin-yang + Framer Motion is the backbone): (1) public/media/concept.mp4 (~6.9MB) on / Threshold as a one-time first-visit ambient hero behind the live SVG; (2) public/media/party.mp4 (~6.1MB) on /sendoff as the single celebratory accent on the drink path; (3) public/media/hangover.mp4 (~2.3MB) optionally on /recover as a calm ambient header. DROP from the core flows the heavy decorative PNGs clarity.png, nottodrink.png, todrink.png, partyfinal.png, reflect.png (~2MB each): they bloat first paint and the designed gradient/SVG UI replaces them; if any are wanted, downscale to WebP under 150KB and treat as optional backgrounds only. Loading rules for ALL video: never block first paint; render the live SVG instantly as the visual, then for video set preload=none, poster=a tiny generated/blurred frame, muted, playsInline, autoPlay only after idle (requestIdleCallback with setTimeout fallback) or on first interaction; load via an IntersectionObserver/lazy wrapper so off-screen routes never fetch it. prefers-reduced-motion or Save-Data -> skip video entirely, show a wine-to-noir CSS gradient. Provide width/height/aspect-ratio to avoid CLS. Re-encode the mp4s to H.264 baseline at a smaller bitrate and ideally add a WebM/HEVC alternative for iOS Safari; they already live in /public outside the JS bundle. Sounds (public/sounds/*.mp3) are OFF by default, lazy-loaded on first enable, gated by the sound toggle; existing swoosh/music are not auto-played. Tao.svg stays as the favicon/app-icon mark.
spec.accessibilityAndCompat: Target iOS Safari + Chrome, 360px small phone to desktop. Use 100svh/100dvh (not 100vh) to dodge the iOS Safari URL-bar resize bug; pad with env(safe-area-inset-*) on all fixed top/bottom chrome (header, bottom tab bar, SOS button) and add viewport-fit=cover to the meta viewport. All tap targets at least 44x44px (the Tailwind touch spacing token is already defined). Respect prefers-reduced-motion via one shared useReducedMotion()/media-query hook that disables loops, parallax, scaling pacers and converts transitions to 150ms-or-less opacity fades; also expose a manual reduced-motion override in Settings. Color contrast at least 4.5:1 for body text on the dark base (verify pearl/secondary text); never rely on color alone for state (pair the bloom/slate/sage dots with icons/labels). Semantic landmarks, focus-visible rings, logical tab order, ESC/back closes modals; the yin-yang carries role=img + a live aria-label of the current Yin% (already present, keep updating it). Live regions announce streak/balance changes politely. Forms (amount, sliders, steppers) have real labels, inputmode=decimal for currency, type=number with min/max for counts; sliders are keyboard-operable. Avoid iOS input zoom by keeping inputs at least 16px font. Honor prefers-color-scheme but default dark. Haptics only via a guarded navigator.vibrate wrapper (existing utils/haptics.js): feature-detect, no-op on iOS Safari, never assume support. PWA/Capacitor-friendly: no browser-only globals on the critical path, service-worker + manifest tested in standalone display mode, local-notification intents stubbed for the future native wrap, offline-first (all assets cacheable, no network dependency).
spec.dataModel: Fully on-device, no network. Primary store: localStorage for small singletons; IndexedDB (via a thin idb wrapper) for the append-only event log so it scales and survives. Keys (all prefixed tt_, versioned). tt_profile_v1 = { onboarded, intent, currency, drinksPerWeekBaseline, spendPerNight, typicalSession, targetYinRatio, rewardGoal:{label,amount}, intention:{trigger,plan}, schemaVersion }. tt_settings_v1 = { sound, haptics, reducedMotionOverride, theme, reminderTime, drinkLimitNudge }. tt_cosmetics_v1 = { ownedSkins[], activeSkin, ownedOutfits[], soundPack }. IndexedDB store 'events' is the single source of truth, append-only, never mutated or deleted on a slip: each event = { id, ts, type, payload }. type in {clear_night, drink_night, urge_surf, soft_landing, mood_checkin, milestone, freeze_used, streak_reset, relapse}. payloads: clear_night {drinksAvoided, moneySaved}; drink_night {drinks, spend, frozen, harmKit}; urge_surf {before, after, note, outcome}; soft_landing {mood, note}; mood_checkin {mood}. DERIVED values are computed by pure memoized selectors over the event log and NEVER stored as authoritative so they cannot drift: currentClearStreak (consecutive local-day clear_night with no drink_night), bestClearStreak (max ever, monotonic), checkinStreak (consecutive days with any event), moneyKept = sum of clear_night.moneySaved (monotonic, never decremented), drinksAvoided = sum of clear_night.drinksAvoided (monotonic), urgesSurfed = count of urge_surf with outcome made_it, clarityPoints = weighted sum (clear +10, recover +15, urge +20, drink +5, checkin +5), yinYangRatio = area-weighted yinActs vs yangActs feeding the chord, clarityTrend = last-30 mood series, calendar heatmap = per-day rollup. Streak Freezes: 2/week + earned, tracked as freeze_used events vs a weekly grant; a freeze on a drink_night prevents the streak pause without deleting history. A streak_reset event marks a Day-1 restart but lifetime sums are unaffected because they read the full log. EXPORT: serialize {profile, settings, cosmetics, events} to pretty-printed JSON Blob downloaded as tequila-tao-balance-YYYY-MM-DD.json. IMPORT: validate schemaVersion, merge/replace, dedupe events by id. CLEAR: double-confirm, wipe all tt_ localStorage keys + the IndexedDB database. All timestamps stored in UTC ms with day-rollups computed in the device's local timezone so nights stay intuitive.
spec.buildNotes: 1) TAILWIND v4 FIX (load-bearing bug): index.css uses @import "tailwindcss" (v4) but the project still ships a v3-style tailwind.config.js (theme.extend with tao colors, screens, fontSize, safe-area padding) that v4 will NOT auto-load, so the custom tokens are currently dead. Either add @config "../tailwind.config.js"; right after the @import to keep the existing JS config, OR (preferred for v4) port the tokens into a CSS-first @theme { --color-tao-wine:#800020; ... } block in index.css and delete the JS config. Pick one and verify the tao-* utilities actually compile. postcss.config.js already uses @tailwindcss/postcss which is correct. 2) REMOVE FIREBASE entirely: delete src/firebase.js, drop firebase from package.json deps (+ lockfile), remove .firebaserc/.firebase/firebase.json/firebase-debug.log, strip any auth/Google-popup code. No login route. 3) ROUTING: main.jsx currently routes /dashboard, /reflect, /party/final, /conundrum, /tao with no guards. Rebuild to the new map (/, /onboarding, /home, /crossroads, /clarity, /sendoff, /urge, /recover, /balance, /progress, /apothecary, /settings, /guide, /privacy, /terms). Add a guard: if no tt_profile_v1.onboarded, redirect / to /onboarding and protect core routes; returning users skip Threshold to /home. Keep React.lazy + Suspense per route (already in place). 4) YINYANG: keep the exact-area bisection but parametrize the hardcoded #fff/#800020/#000 to CSS-variable-driven skin colors, swap the straight chord for the animated S-curve path, add target chord + seed-dots + patina rim; expose value (yang fraction), target, breath, patina, skin, onSeedTap props. 5) Add a shared useReducedMotion hook and route ALL Framer Motion variants through it; remove the 720-1080deg swirl transitions. 6) Build a small data layer module (selectors + IndexedDB wrapper) so screens read derived stats from one place; never store derived totals as authoritative. 7) Fix yin-yang render: ensure the divider/dots scale correctly at 360px and as a maskable PWA icon; verify aria-label updates with value. 8) PWA: manifest.json needs proper raster icons (192/512 + maskable) in addition to Tao.svg, add viewport-fit=cover, register a service worker for offline (precache app shell + lazy-cache media), keep start_url /. 9) Lazy-wrap all video/sound behind IntersectionObserver + idle + reduced-motion/Save-Data checks. 10) Money/spend stored as real currency numbers (not cents-as-points); kill the legacy opaque constants (yin1=150000, temp-yin 200000, yang skip 500000, drink 100000). 11) Reconsider heavy react-hot-toast usage if it conflicts with the no-red calm tone; prefer in-component feedback. 12) Add a tabular-nums utility to all stat numbers.
openRisks: iOS Safari autoplay/codec: the existing mp4s may not autoplay or may stutter on Safari; needs muted+playsInline, re-encode to a Safari-friendly profile and ideally an HEVC/WebM alt. Verify on a real device, and the gradient fallback must be genuinely acceptable since video may simply not play.

Total media weight (~3 videos + ~5 PNGs, roughly 25MB) vs offline precache budget: must decide what the service worker precaches vs lazy-caches so first load and the PWA install stay light; large media can blow Cache/IndexedDB quotas on low-end devices.
Tailwind v4 config migration is easy to get subtly wrong (utilities silently not generated); needs a visual smoke test that tao-* tokens render before building screens on top of them.

localStorage/IndexedDB can be evicted by iOS (7-day eviction for non-installed PWAs) or in private mode; the export/import backup is the only safety net, so proactively nudge users to install the PWA and to export, and handle storage-unavailable gracefully.
Day-rollup timezone/DST edge cases for streaks (travel, midnight boundaries, clock changes) can produce surprising streak breaks; needs careful local-day computation and tests.
Animated SVG path-morphing the S-curve + breathing + dots on every screen could cost battery/FPS on low-end phones; profile and ensure reduced-motion and idle-pausing genuinely cut the work.

Scope: 15 routes plus gamification, collectibles and a shop is large for a v2; ship the core loop first (Threshold, Onboarding, Home, Crossroads, Clarity, Sendoff, Urge, Recover, Balance, Settings, legal) and treat Apothecary/collectibles/share-cards as a fast-follow.

Tone risk: the gamification (cards, levels, shop) must never trivialize addiction or feel manipulative; keep urge-surfing the most-rewarded action, keep all lifetime stats monotonic, and user-test the copy with the target audience.
Capacitor wrap later: local notifications, haptics and file export behave differently in the native shell than the browser; the web stubs need to be swappable without rearchitecting.

content.title: Privacy Policy
content.markdown:
# Privacy Policy

**Last updated: June 22, 2026**

## The short version

This app helps you on your de-addiction journey, and it does that entirely on your own device. There are no accounts to create, no servers to sign in to, and no analytics running in the background. Everything you enter stays with you. We don't collect it, we don't see it, and we can't upload it — because there's nowhere for it to go.

## What we collect

Nothing. We don't collect any personal information.

The app has no user accounts, no login, and no email sign-up. We never ask for your name, phone number, or location.

## Where your data lives

All the information you create in the app — your check-ins, streaks, notes, goals, settings, and progress — is stored **locally on your device** in the app's own storage.

This data never leaves your device automatically. It isn't sent to us, to a cloud, or to any third party. There is no hidden sync.

## No tracking, no analytics

By default, the app does not include:

- Analytics or usage tracking
- Advertising or ad identifiers
- Third-party trackers or social media pixels
- Crash reporting that sends data off your device

We don't build a profile of you, and we don't follow what you do.

## Optional anonymous, local-only state

You can use the app with a fully anonymous, local-only profile. That means any identity, label, or progress data exists only on your device and is not tied to a real-world identity or any online account. It stays anonymous and stays local.

## You're in control: export and wipe

Your data is yours, and you can take it or remove it at any time:

- **Export** — Save a copy of your data to your device so you can back it up or move it yourself.
- **Wipe** — Permanently delete all your data from the app. Once you wipe it, it's gone. Since we never had a copy, there's nothing for us to delete on our end and nothing for us to recover.

Uninstalling the app also removes its local data, depending on how your device handles app storage.

## Permissions

If the app ever asks for a device permission (for example, notifications or file access for export), it's used only to make that feature work on your device. Permissions are not used to collect or transmit your data.

## Children's privacy

This app is intended for general audiences seeking support. It does not knowingly collect personal information from anyone, including children, because it does not collect personal information at all.

## Third parties

Because the app runs on-device and doesn't upload your data, we don't share or sell your information to anyone — there's nothing to share or sell.

If your device's operating system (such as your phone's app store or backup service) handles app data through its own settings, that's governed by **that** provider's privacy policy, not this one. You can manage those backups in your device settings.

## Changes to this policy

If we ever change how the app handles data, we'll update this policy and change the "Last updated" date at the top. If a future version of the app adds an optional feature that sends data somewhere, we'll make that clear and keep it opt-in.

## Contact

Questions about this policy? Reach out at **kumar.ishaan93@gmail.com**.

---

*This privacy policy is provided for general informational purposes to explain how the app works. It is not legal advice. Privacy laws vary by region and change over time, so if you need a policy tailored to your specific legal obligations, please consult a qualified attorney.*

content.title: Terms of Use
content.markdown:
# Terms of Use

**Last updated: 2026-06-22**

Hi, and welcome. We built this app to support people who are working to cut back on or step away from a substance or habit. We're really glad you're here. Before you dive in, please read these terms. We've tried to keep them plain and honest.

By using the app, you're agreeing to what's below. If you don't agree, that's okay — but please don't use the app.

---

## Please read first: This is not medical advice

**This app is not a doctor, therapist, counselor, or any other healthcare professional, and it is not a substitute for one.**

Everything in the app — the content, tools, trackers, tips, check-ins, and any messages — is for general informational and self-support purposes only. It is **not** medical, clinical, psychological, or treatment advice. It is not a diagnosis, a treatment plan, or care.

- Do not use this app in place of seeing a qualified clinician.
- Do not start, stop, or change any medication, treatment, or care plan based on anything in this app.
- Always talk to a licensed healthcare professional about your situation before making decisions about your health or recovery.

If something in the app ever seems to conflict with advice from your doctor or care team, **follow your doctor or care team.**

---

## Important safety note about alcohol and other dependence

This one matters, so we want to be direct.

**Stopping alcohol suddenly can be dangerous — sometimes life-threatening.** If your body has become dependent on alcohol, quitting "cold turkey" without medical support can cause serious withdrawal symptoms, including seizures and a condition called delirium tremens (DTs). The same caution applies to some other substances, such as benzodiazepines (sedatives) and opioids.

**Please do not use this app as a reason to suddenly stop drinking or stop using a substance on your own.** Talk to a clinician first. A doctor can help you taper or detox safely, often with medication and monitoring. Reducing safely with support is not a failure — it's the smart, medically sound way to do it.

If you are already experiencing withdrawal symptoms (shaking, sweating, racing heart, confusion, hallucinations, or seizures), **treat it as an emergency and get medical help right away.**

---

## If you're in crisis

If you are thinking about harming yourself, are in danger, or are facing a medical emergency, **please reach out for real help now — not this app.**

- **Call your local emergency number** (for example, 911 in the US and Canada, 112 across much of Europe and many other countries, 999 in the UK, 000 in Australia, or your country's equivalent).
- **Contact a crisis or suicide-prevention helpline in your country.** Most countries have one, and many are free, confidential, and available 24/7. If you're not sure where to look, the directory at **findahelpline.com** and the International Association for Suicide Prevention list at **iasp.info/resources/Crisis_Centres** can point you to a line near you.
- **In the US**, you can call or text **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
- **Reach out to someone you trust** — a friend, family member, doctor, or local emergency service.

You deserve support from a real person. Please don't wait.

---

## What you can expect from us (and what you can't)

This app is provided **free of charge** and **"as is."** We offer it to be helpful, but we can't make promises about results.

- We don't guarantee the app will help you cut back, quit, stay sober, or reach any particular goal.
- We don't guarantee the app will always be accurate, complete, up to date, available, or error-free.
- Recovery is personal and complex. What works is different for everyone, and no app can replace human care and connection.

---

## Acceptable use

We ask you to use the app honestly and kindly. Please **do not**:

- Use the app for anything illegal, harmful, or abusive.
- Use it to harass, threaten, impersonate, or harm other people.
- Rely on it for medical, legal, or emergency decisions (see the sections above).
- Try to break, hack, overload, reverse-engineer, or disrupt the app or its systems.
- Scrape, copy, resell, or redistribute the app or its content without our permission.
- Misrepresent the app as professional treatment or advice to others.

If you use the app in a way that's harmful or breaks these terms, we may limit or stop your access.

You're responsible for how you use the app and for any decisions you make. Please use good judgment, and lean on real professionals for the big stuff.

---

## Your content and privacy (the short version)

Anything you log or write in the app is yours. Please be thoughtful about what you share, and remember that no app or service can promise perfect security. For details on how we handle your information, see our Privacy Policy.

---

## No warranty

To the fullest extent allowed by law, the app is provided **"as is" and "as available," without warranties of any kind**, whether express or implied. That includes (but isn't limited to) any implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, reliability, and non-infringement.

We do not warrant that the app will meet your needs, be uninterrupted, be secure, or be free of errors, bugs, or harmful components.

Some places don't allow certain warranty exclusions, so part of this section may not apply to you.

---

## Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent allowed by law, the people and organization behind this app will **not be liable** for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, or for any loss of health outcomes, profits, data, goodwill, or other intangible losses, arising out of or related to your use of (or inability to use) the app.

Because this app is provided free of charge and "as is," **our total liability to you for any and all claims is limited to the amount you paid to use it — which is zero.**

This includes anything that happens as a result of relying on the app instead of seeking professional or emergency help. **Please always seek professional and emergency help when you need it.**

Some places don't allow certain liability limits, so part of this section may not apply to you. Nothing in these terms limits liability that can't legally be limited.

---

## Changes to these terms

We may update these terms from time to time as the app evolves. When we do, we'll change the "Last updated" date at the top. If the changes are significant, we'll try to give you a heads-up in the app. Continuing to use the app after an update means you accept the new terms.

---

## Wrapping up

We're rooting for you. This app is one small tool — use it alongside real people, real support, and real professional care. You don't have to do this alone, and reaching out for help is a sign of strength, not weakness.

Take care of yourself.

If you have questions about these terms, you can reach us through the contact option in the app.

content.title: Tequila Tao — Guide / How It Works
content.markdown:
# How It Works

Welcome. This is the part where we tell you exactly how everything works — no mystery math, no hidden points, no judgment. Just you, a breathing circle, and the truth about your own nights.

---

## The Yin-Yang (the whole idea in one screen)

That circle at the center isn't a score. It's a portrait of your relationship with drinking, drawn live.

- **Yin** (the pale half) = clarity, rest, restraint, recovery, money kept, drinks avoided.
- **Yang** (the warm half) = celebration, indulgence, the nights you *choose* to drink.

Here's the part most apps get wrong: **a fully pale circle is also out of balance.** This isn't a purity meter. The Taoist goal is *mindful agency* — choosing well — not white-knuckle perfection. That's why a thin seed of yang always stays. You're allowed joy.

**Your win is not an all-white circle.** Your win is the curve drifting toward *your* dashed target line — the balance **you** picked in onboarding (say 85/15) — and getting gracefully back to it after any night that pulls it the other way.

### The two seed-dots
The little dots inside each half are real, and you can tap them.

- **The light dot inside yang** = the one clear thing you keep even on a big night (water between drinks, a safe ride home). Tap it on a drink night to make that promise — it glows until morning.
- **The dark dot inside yin** = permission to celebrate. You're allowed to want a good time.

> They're also Tao the mascot's eyes. Long-press the yin dot for a quiet koan when you need one.

---

## What each action does (the real numbers)

No multipliers, no secrets. Here's the entire economy.

### Logging a Clear Night
You chose not to drink when you might have. Each clear night gives you:
- **+1 to your Clear-Night Streak**
- **+ Drinks Avoided** (your typical session size — default 3, editable)
- **+ Money Kept** (your typical spend, prefilled, editable per night)
- **+1 Yin notch** — the curve springs toward your target

All four numbers show *live* before you confirm. One choice, four real-world wins.

### Surfing an urge (the most-rewarded thing you can do)
Cravings crest and pass like waves — usually in about 20 minutes. Ride one out without drinking and you get:
- **+1 Urge Surfed** (lifetime and this-week — the pride metric)
- A **before/after intensity rating** so you literally *see* it fall (8 → 3) on a tiny chart
- **A Yin notch** (about half a clear night)

We reward this more than anything else on purpose. Proving to yourself that the wave passes is the single strongest predictor of lasting change.

### Choosing to drink (logged honestly)
No shame screen. No red. Nothing breaks here.
- Your streak **pauses — it does not reset to zero.** Your best streak is safe; a fresh count begins.
- You get **2 free Freezes a week**, so a *planned* night doesn't even have to pause it.
- We log drinks + spend so your trends stay honest.
- We offer a one-tap **Harm-Reduction Kit** and the keep-one-clear-thing promise.
- **Your lifetime Money Kept and Drinks Avoided never go down.** Ever.

Honesty has to be safe, or you'll stop being honest. So it is.

### A morning-after Recover (Soft Landing)
Self-care literally grows your yin. Completing a recovery session gives **+1 Soft Landing**, a Yin notch, and folds a quick clarity check-in into your trend. The notches are for the *care* — never for the drinking. Rough morning? That's data, not failure.

### Daily mood check-in (one tap)
Paints a tile in your Clarity Garden and feeds your 7-day trend. This streak stays alive **even on drink nights** — showing up always counts. Miss a day? No penalty.

### Milestones
At 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 100 clear nights (and money/drinks thresholds), the rim earns one **gold patina arc** — like respect aging into old brass. No confetti. Just a single soft bloom, a real-numbers milestone card, and your level title rising: **Sprout → Stream → Grove → Mountain → Still Lake.**

---

## The balance math (fully transparent)

You can see this anytime on the Balance screen. That's the whole formula:

- Clear night → **+3 yin**
- Each drink → **+1 yang**
- Urge surfed → **+1 yin**
- Soft Landing → **+1 yin**

Your Yin% is the area-weighted balance of those acts. That's it. Nothing hidden, nothing you can't audit.

---

## Tonight: Party / Intentions mode

Tap **Tonight** to reach the Crossroads — your nightly choice, calm and pressure-free.

1. We ask how you're feeling about tonight (1–10). Feeling it strongly? We'll gently offer 3 minutes with the Urge tool first.
2. Two equal panels, no guilt styling: **Not tonight** and **I'm having some.** Each shows a live preview of what it'll do.
3. We remind you of the intention you set in onboarding (*when X, I will Y*).
4. **Not tonight** → bank the win on the Clarity screen.
5. **I'm having some** → a friendly Send-off: log your count, optionally **Freeze your streak**, flip on Harm-Reduction toggles (water between rounds, set a limit, eat first, safe ride), and make your one clear promise.
6. **Not sure?** routes you to the urge tool — ambivalence gets a tool, never a lecture.

> Send-off is warm, not preachy. "Have a good one. I'll be here in the morning, no judgment."

---

## Recover: morning-after mode

Tap **Morning after** for a Soft Landing — a gentle, de-jargoned checklist, each item with a quick *why*:

- Hydrate · Box-breathing (inline 60s guide) · Light fuel · Caffeine timing · Safety check · optional Cooling/Movement

Every tick fills a ring and quietly fills your yin field. A **safety card stays front and center** — severe withdrawal symptoms mean *seek real care* (this is the one place we use alarm colors, because it matters). End with a 1–5 mood check and an optional "what I learned." You finish with +1 Soft Landing and a yin notch.

This isn't damage control. It's a legitimate act of clarity.

---

## Urge support (Stillpoint / SOS)

The SOS button is always one tap away, works fully offline, and never disappears.

1. Rate the urge (1–10).
2. Breathe with the full-screen pacer — same circle that powers the app's idle breath — through a 3–5 minute wave timer. "Urges crest in ~20 minutes." Skippable to 90 seconds.
3. Grounding prompts rotate by ("You're the surfer, not the wave"), with quick exits: call someone, walk, water.
4. Rate it again — and watch the drop on the chart.
5. Outcome: **Made it** (+Urge Surfed, yin notch) or **I'm choosing to drink** (straight to Send-off, zero shame).

---

## Slips are data, not defeat

If a streak breaks, you'll see soft slate — never alarm-red, never "you failed." You'll get a calm **Welcome back, the water's fine.**

- Your **14 best days still happened.** Your best streak stays.
- One tap restarts the clear-night count at Day 1, with an optional "what did I learn."
- You earn a gentle **Back to the Water** card — because returning *is* part of the path.
- Untouched: your check-in streak, Money Kept, Drinks Avoided, Urges Surfed, and best streak.

These lifetime numbers only ever go up. A slip can't erase them. That's the point.

---

## The philosophy

This app runs on two beliefs:

- **Mindful agency over forced abstinence.** The goal is to *choose* your nights on purpose and steer toward the balance you set — not to chase a perfect white circle.
- **Harm reduction over shame.** Punishing honesty teaches people to lie. So we never say you failed. We say: *the balance shifted — what do you want next?*

Big night? "Let's land it well tomorrow." That's the whole tone. Curious, warm, never scolding.

---

## A few quiet touches

- The circle **breathes at ~6 breaths a minute** — a stealth calm-down pacer hiding as a logo.
- **Money Kept** reframes playfully on tap ("that's a concert ticket / 0.3 of a flight home"), tied to the reward *you* chose.
- Wins drop **Clarity Cards** to collect; milestones make a share-card image that never outs you as a recovery app unless you want it to.
- **Tao** the mascot is dry and kind: *"I counted your clear nights. I lost count. Good problem."*

---

## Privacy & care

**No account. No servers. No tracking.** Everything lives on your device. Export a backup anytime — and please do, so a slip of your phone storage never costs you your history.

**This app is not medical care.** Severe alcohol withdrawal can be dangerous. If you're unwell, seek professional help. Crisis and helpline resources are linked in Settings.

*Two forces. One you. Find your balance, one clear night at a time.*

content.title: The Tao of Tequila Tao — About & Philosophy
content.markdown:
# The Tao of Tequila Tao

*Two forces. One you. Find your balance, one clear night at a time.*

## Why a yin-yang?

Because you are not a number, and you are definitely not a six-figure point blob glowing in an app you don't understand.

You are a person with a complicated, very human relationship to drinking — some nights you want the quiet, some nights you want the party, and most nights you just want to feel like the choice was actually yours. The yin-yang holds both. Yin is the clear water: rest, restraint, money kept, the morning that doesn't hurt. Yang is the warm fire: celebration, the night you *choose*, the joy you're allowed to have.

Neither side is the good guy.

That's the part most apps get wrong. A purity meter tells you the goal is a perfectly white circle and then quietly shames you back to Day 1 every time you're human. We don't do that here. An all-yin circle is out of balance too. There's always a small seed of fire kept inside the water — because the Tao isn't forced abstinence, it's **mindful agency**. The water doesn't try to flow. It just does.

## What "winning" actually means

You set your own balance. In onboarding you drag the chord to *your* target — maybe 85/15, maybe something else entirely — and that dashed marker becomes your north star. Winning is the curve drifting toward your mark. Winning is the comeback after a heavy night. Winning is showing up.

Over consistent days the divider settles softly toward center, like still water finding its level, and the rim earns a thin arc of gold — patina, the way respect collects on old brass. No confetti. We don't celebrate you the way a slot machine does. We let the symbol quietly age into something that looks like the steadiness you built.

## The two seeds

Tap the bright seed inside the fire and you make one clear promise for a night you're drinking — water between rounds, a safe ride home — the one clear thing you keep even on a big night. It glows until morning.

Long-press the dark seed inside the water for a koan, because permission to celebrate lives in there too. You are allowed joy. That has never been the thing we're trying to take from you.

## A big night is not a failure

We will never tell you that you failed. If the night gets heavy, the curve bows gently toward the fire and we get curious instead of cruel: *Big night. Let's land it well tomorrow.* Your best streak is preserved, your money kept and drinks avoided never tick down, and the morning after is its own small, honorable act of care — not damage control, just clarity, growing.

Slips are data, not defeat. The water's fine. Come on back.

---

## An honest note: this is not professional care

Tequila Tao is a private, on-device companion for building a more mindful relationship with drinking. It is a thoughtful friend with a calendar and a calculator. It is **not** a doctor, a therapist, a treatment program, or a medical device — and it can't be, and it doesn't pretend to be.

It cannot diagnose anything. It cannot manage withdrawal. It does not know your full story, your health, or your risk. Everything here is encouragement and reflection, offered as-is, with no warranty and no clinical authority.

If anything in your body or your life suggests you need real care, please trust that over anything an app tells you.

## Please reach out if drinking feels out of control

There is no shame in this app, and there's no shame in this either. Reaching for help is the most yin thing you can do — the soft overcoming the hard.

Consider talking to someone if you notice:

- You can't cut down or stop even when you genuinely want to
- Drinking is costing you relationships, work, sleep, or money in ways that scare you
- You drink to manage shaking, sweating, nausea, anxiety, or to steady your hands in the morning
- You feel you need to drink, rather than choosing to

**A serious safety word on withdrawal:** if you're a heavy daily drinker, stopping suddenly can be genuinely dangerous. Tremors, confusion, hallucinations, seizures, racing heart, or a high fever are medical emergencies. Do not try to white-knuckle that alone — seek medical care.

**If you're in crisis or thinking about harming yourself, contact your local emergency number right now.** For ongoing support, reach out to a doctor, a counselor, or a substance-use helpline in your region. (Region-aware helpline links live in the Guide and Terms pages inside the app.)

You can keep using Tequila Tao alongside real care — they're not in competition. The strongest, calmest move you can make is to let a real human help carry this with you.

We'll be here in the morning. No judgment.
